name: scenarioforge
description: ScenarioForge — scenario-spined plugin suite for Claude Code. BMAD 4-phase pipeline (Analysis -> Planning -> Solutioning -> Implementation) with an orchestrator + 3-tier worker architecture, where a business scenario is the aggregate root that every phase traces back to.

plugins.name: scenario-discovery
plugins.description: Phase 1 (Analysis). Turns raw user requirements into a traceable scenarios.json — the spine that every later phase references. Human supplies business intent; AI agents enrich the analysis layer but cannot commit. Includes a beat-1.5 multi-persona ideation panel (registry-driven and expandable; personas can run on external OpenAI-compatible AI providers or on local AI CLIs — codex/opencode/gemini/claude — for model diversity without extra API cost) and event-first (Event Storming) elicitation.

plugins.name: domain-design
plugins.description: Phase 2 (Planning). Reads each scenario's business + domain_concepts and produces the domain model — entities, Data Dictionary, use cases, API contracts, sitemap — writing traces_down back to the spine. Scale-adaptive, with a /deliver-docs command that assembles the full 10-section System Design Document for handoff.

plugins.name: screen-binding
plugins.description: Phase 2 (Planning, UI). Theme-first: builds a Bootstrap 5 master page + theme.css + navbar + menu nav from the sitemap as viewable HTML, then mocks each has_ui screen as a wireframe or a high-fidelity HTML page (via the frontend-design skill). Talks to Claude Design both ways — import a handoff bundle or generate per-screen prompts — and binds every page back via traces_down.pages. Renamed from ui-mockup.

plugins.name: solution-arch
plugins.description: Phase 3 (Solutioning). Reads each scenario's traces_down (entities + apis from domain-design, pages from screen-binding) and composes feature units (FE-xxx) with their layering — controller, service/handler, repository, DI, DTOs — into features.json, then writes traces_down.features back to the spine. Says HOW the design assembles; never invents entities or writes code. Scale-adaptive (QUICK/STANDARD/ENTERPRISE).

plugins.name: feature-builder
plugins.description: Phase 4 (Implementation). The Tier 2 agentic worker (renamed from long-running). Picks ready features from features.json and builds each into working code (ASP.NET Core 8 MVC + EF Core + DDD), looping build -> fix until green. Acts as a per-feature dispatcher: routes hard / cross-page / security-sensitive features to an Opus subagent and clusters of simple, similar features to an Opus-built exemplar + Sonnet replicas, to cut cost without losing quality. Runs an 8-step verification pipeline (build, design compliance, CRUD, API integration, test coverage, tech audit, config, Scenario Trace Check) plus a UI Control Manifest gate on every feature, writing traces_down.features back to the spine. Resumable via a progress ledger; circuit-breaker bounded with Sonnet->Opus escalation. Provides /implement, /route, /continue, /status, /verify, /retry, /gaps, /help commands.

plugins.name: scenario-verify
plugins.description: Phase 4 (QA). The end-of-spine prover (renamed from qa-ui-test). Reads each scenario's acceptance criteria plus the UI Control Manifests feature-builder emitted, and derives traceable E2E test scenarios (TS-xxx) into qa-tracker.json — Layer 2 of the two-layer fence. For every form control it generates the 5 mandatory test categories (render-binding, api-binding, permission, validation, cascade-loading-error), each carrying a scenario_ref so a test always traces back to the business scenario it proves. Generation is a deterministic Tier 1 pass; running the suite is a Tier 2 agentic loop (run -> debug -> retry through Playwright until each scenario goes green or a circuit breaker bounds it). Acts as a per-category dispatcher: permission / cascade -> Opus, api-binding / validation -> Sonnet, render-binding -> Haiku. Enforces Gate 4 (control coverage) — every control x mandatory category must have a passing scenario before release. Writes traces_down.test_scenarios back to the spine; resumable via the qa-tracker ledger. Provides /generate, /run, /route, /continue, /retest, /status, /coverage, /edit, /gaps, /help commands.

plugins.name: orchestrator
plugins.description: Tier 0 coordinator — the single entry point that drives the whole pipeline. Reads the scenario spine (scenarios.json), decides the effort scale, plans the phase sequence (Analysis -> Planning -> Planning/UI -> Solutioning -> Implementation -> QA), and delegates each phase to its worker with a 4-part contract — without writing any artifact itself. Between phases it runs a verify gate that checks the finished worker's handoff against the spine before the next phase may start; a red gate stops the line (re-delegate once for a worker shortfall, or surface an upstream gap for the user to resolve). Sequential delegation (each phase consumes the last's output), flat hierarchy (the orchestrator spawns workers; a worker never spawns a worker), circuit-breaker bounded (a per-run cap on delegations and gate retries). Resumable across sessions via a run ledger. Provides /build, /plan, /next, /status, /gate, /resume, /scale, /help commands.
